title: Eurybia schreberi
taxonomy: Eurybia schreberi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Eurybia and is native to North America. The classification of Eurybia schreberi is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Asterales. The plant's classification is a result of its unique characteristics and relationships to other plants within the Asteraceae family. Eurybia schreberi is a part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms.
uses: Eurybia schreberi, also known as New York aster, has been used in traditional medicine for its various properties. Eurybia schreberi has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Its medicinal uses include treating wounds, skin conditions, and possibly reducing fever. In folk medicine, it was used to treat coughs and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.

intro: Eurybia schreberi, also known as American mountain mint, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. The bioactive constituents of Eurybia schreberi include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, American mountain mint is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to relieve digestive discomfort and promote overall well-being.
